topic: SAA Formation ~950 AD (matches Satan's little season ~1000 AD)
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 306 to 1329 (+334%); detail_evidence_length 1063 to 1527 (+44%); finding_length 269 to 339 (+26%); verdict unchanged (Unfalsifiable). Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-05T20:52Z (age 70h, valid). obe_skipped=8 (WIN-050/028/009/036/060/018/059/057 already re-reviewed since audit). Re-review triggered by detail_verdict_text expansion: U-category templated post-hoc paragraph added per ISS-820/EXP-132 batch. Cycle 3.
our_argument_summary: WIN-023 claims the South Atlantic Anomaly formed around 950 AD and that this matches Revelation 20's 'Satan's little season ~1000 AD' in a full-preterist reading. Our review accepts the ~950 AD date as published paleomagnetic science (Campuzano et al. 2019 SHAWQ2k; corroborated by Tarduno 2015 and Hare 2018 GRL on prior SAA-like recurrences) but classifies the WIN as Unfalsifiable because (a) the date is post-hoc adopted from external literature rather than derived from dome geometry, (b) the dome provides no physical mechanism linking dome parameters to any specific date, (c) the theological link is a categorical assertion with no observable to confirm or refute, and (d) given Hare-style recurrence intervals (~200-400 yr) and archaeomagnetic dating uncertainty (±50-100 yr), the 50-year gap between ~950 AD and the theological ~1000 AD target falls within noise and would have hit some recurrence regardless of which target century was chosen.

relevant_files: raw-text/06-evolution.txt (admits 'failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting or predictive tellurics')
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: There is no calculation pipeline. The dome site lists 'WIN-023 — SAA Formation ~950 AD CONFIRMED' as a static claim with the parenthetical '(matches Satan's little season ~1000 AD)'. No formula, no derivation from disc_radius / firmament_height / aetheric parameters connects dome geometry to any date. The only programmatic mention of WIN-023 in the dome's pipeline is a static entry asserting confirmed status (per the c2 review, in inject_ai_layer.py's confirmed_wins array — neither monitor.py nor pull_data.py is included in the current raw-text/ snapshot, so we cannot independently re-verify their absence of a WIN-023 domain in this cycle, but the c2 finding stands and the dome's own evolution.txt admits failure to mathematically reconcile this).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — there is no pred==obs comparison at all. The 'CONFIRMED' label is asserted, not computed.
data_sources_used: None internal to the dome pipeline. The ~950 AD date originates externally in Campuzano et al. 2019 (EPSL, SHAWQ2k geomagnetic field reconstruction).
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The categorical/theological framing IS the escape clause: any claim involving 'Satan's little season' has no observable failure mode. The dome cannot be wrong about WIN-023 because there is nothing to be wrong about.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our detail_verdict_text says 'monitor.py contains no validation domain for this WIN, confirming that CONFIRMED is self-awarded rather than computationally verified.' That assertion is correct under the c2-validated description (no domain, no fetch, no comparison). However, the code_analysis_tags.monitoring field currently says 'hardcoded', which the prompt's tag-definition glossary reserves for 'monitor.py contains a static expected value AND a static observed value... pred==obs with both sides baked in.' A bare 'confirmed' assertion in inject_ai_layer.py with no domain in monitor.py is the 'none' category, not 'hardcoded'. The tag and the verdict-text description are mutually inconsistent.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Per c2 review and confirmed by current snapshot search, no domain in monitor.py fetches paleomagnetic data or runs a pred==obs check on the ~950 AD date. The only programmatic mention is a static confirmed-status entry (inject_ai_layer.py per c2). Per the prompt's tag definitions: 'hardcoded' requires both a static expected value AND a static observed value with pred==obs logic; absence of a comparison is 'none'. Recommend correcting code_analysis.monitoring from 'hardcoded' to 'none' in data/wins.json. This also better matches the detail_verdict_text wording 'no validation domain for this WIN'.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard geophysics explains SAA formation via core-mantle boundary reverse-flux patches (LLSVP-influenced flow, Tarduno/Campuzano/Hare). The dome does NOT rename this with aetheric vocabulary; it adds a parallel theological interpretation (Revelation 20 timeline). This is an unfalsifiable overlay, not a vocabulary substitution. Tag remains false (correct).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Campuzano et al. 2019 published the ~950 AD date in EPSL well before the dome model adopted it; Tarduno 2015 (Nature Comms) and Hare 2018 (GRL DOI 10.1002/2017GL076007) predate dome incorporation. The dome literally lifted the date from published literature and labeled it confirmed.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation path connects disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude=5733 km, moon_altitude=2534 km, or any aetheric parameter to the date 950 AD. The dome's own 06-evolution.txt admits 'completely failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting or predictive tellurics'.

holes_found.description: TLDR_EVIDENCE FACTUALLY WRONG ABOUT THEOLOGICAL REFERENT. tldr_evidence says 'Linking it to a theological timeline (Tower of Babel) adds no testable prediction.' The dome's actual theological referent is NOT the Tower of Babel — it is 'Satan's little season ~1000 AD' from a full-preterist reading of Revelation 20:7-10 (per dome 02-wins.txt and 07-ai-context.txt). Tower of Babel is Genesis 11 with no specific date and no presence in the dome's WIN-023 framing — a search of the entire raw-text/ snapshot returns zero hits for 'Babel'. Per the prompt's TLDR severity guidance ('TLDR factual errors are major; readers see TLDRs first and may not expand'), a wrong theological referent in the TLDR is a major hole — a Christian reader who knows Babel ≠ Revelation 20 will dismiss the entire WIN review on this single error. Worse, the wrong referent makes our argument inadvertently weaker: Babel has no date in scripture, so 'no testable prediction' from a Babel link is trivially true; but Satan's-little-season DOES have a textual time anchor (1000-year reign of Christ, then a 'little season') and a date the dome explicitly numbers as ~1000 AD, which is what makes the dome's pattern-match feel substantive even though it doesn't survive scrutiny. The TLDR is fighting a strawman of the dome's actual claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'Tower of Babel' with 'Satan's little season (Revelation 20)' in tldr_evidence. Suggested rewrite: 'The ~950 AD date for the SAA's formation comes from published paleomagnetic studies — the dome didn't discover it. Linking it to a theological timeline (Revelation 20's Satan's little season ~1000 AD) adds no testable prediction; the dome provides no mechanism deriving the date from its geometry. Given recurrence intervals of ~200-400 years and dating uncertainty of ±50-100 years, some recurrence within 50 years of any pre-chosen target was statistically near-certain.' This also drops the 'millions of years' claim (see next hole).

holes_found.description: TLDR_EVIDENCE 'MILLIONS OF YEARS' CLAIM IS UNSUPPORTED BY DETAIL_EVIDENCE. tldr_evidence says 'Earlier SAA-like features existed millions of years ago, undermining the singular-origin narrative.' The detail_evidence cites only Hare et al. 2018 ~400-700 AD (centuries, not millions of years) and Campuzano 2019 ~950 AD. There IS published Myr-scale support for long-lived South Atlantic anomalous behavior (Tarduno et al. 2020 Saint Helena dispersion, PNAS DOI 10.1073/pnas.2001217117; Trindade speleothem PNAS DOI 10.1073/pnas.1809197115), but neither is cited in WIN-023's detail_evidence. The TLDR is making a claim the detail can't back up — exactly the dome-style pattern we criticize.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) drop the 'millions of years' clause from tldr_evidence as part of the rewrite above, OR (b) add a sentence to detail_evidence citing Tarduno et al. 2020 (Saint Helena, DOI 10.1073/pnas.2001217117) and/or Trindade et al. 2018 (speleothem, DOI 10.1073/pnas.1809197115) for the Myr-scale recurrence and update the TLDR to reference 'recurrent for millennia or longer'. Option (a) is simpler and stays within current evidence base; option (b) is stronger but requires a citation_check pass on the new DOIs.

holes_found.description: INTERNAL TAG INCONSISTENCY: code_analysis.monitoring='hardcoded' contradicts detail_verdict_text. The detail_verdict_text says 'The dome's own monitoring system (monitor.py) contains no validation domain for this WIN, confirming that CONFIRMED is self-awarded rather than computationally verified.' That description matches the prompt's 'none' definition (no domain, no fetch, no comparison). The 'hardcoded' tag is reserved for cases where monitor.py contains static pred AND static obs values with a comparison. A bare 'confirmed' assertion with no domain at all should be 'none'. Aggregate Part 2b counts (e.g., {{CA_HARDCODED}} in sections.json) will be off by one until corrected, and a defender comparing the prose claim to the tag column will note the discrepancy.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch data/wins.json WIN-023 code_analysis.monitoring from 'hardcoded' to 'none'. The c2 monitoring_evidence already says 'static declaration of confirmed status' which is the 'none' description in the c2 reviewer's own words; the tag was set inconsistently with the evidence text in c2 and has carried forward. This is the exact failure mode flagged in the prompt's WIN-014 callout ('never assume the previous reviewer got it right').

holes_found.description: FINDING LENGTH 339 CHARS — verbosity persisted/grew despite c2 recommendation. c2 recommended tightening from 269 chars to a one-liner. Current finding is 339 chars (longer). However, the growth was substantive: ISS-446 patched in the kernel-of-truth ('the dome correctly identified a real paleomagnetic event') and the Schumann-resonance attribution ('then wrongly attributes the timing to Schumann resonance changes via an unfalsifiable aetheric phase transition'). This adds genuine analytical content. The finding is now a defensible compromise between the c2 brevity recommendation and the ISS-446 kernel-of-truth requirement. NOT recommending another tightening pass — the verbose finding is more honest than a snappy one-liner that strawmans.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. Document this resolution so future curmudgeon cycles don't re-litigate length: tightening would now require dropping either the kernel-of-truth or the Schumann-attribution, and both are load-bearing for the verdict. Mark the c2 length-hole as superseded_by_iss446 in any tracker that still carries it.

holes_found.description: POST-HOC TEMPLATE PARAGRAPH IN DETAIL_VERDICT_TEXT IS LOAD-BEARING BUT VERY GENERIC. The new ~1023-char paragraph added by EXP-132 ('Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test...') is the U-category template applied to WIN-023. The template-generic phrasing ('this is not a derived quantity that can be checked against measurement; it is a categorical assertion') is correct but reads as boilerplate when paired with the highly specific opening paragraph. A defender could call it 'the auto-generated bit'. The template content is correct — the criticism is rhetorical.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish: open the second paragraph with a one-sentence connector that ties the generic claim back to WIN-023 specifically. E.g., 'The same logic applies categorically here: linking ~950 AD to a theological timeline...' would soften the template-jump. Not blocking; the verdict text functions correctly as written.

stronger_arguments.description: Compute and cite the noise-floor probability explicitly. Using the c2 statistical sketch with concrete numbers — Hare-style recurrence period ~300 yr, combined dating+target uncertainty ~±75 yr, hit-window ~200 yr per recurrence — the probability that SOME paleomagnetic recurrence falls within 50 years of any pre-chosen Western-eschatology date (~1000 AD, ~70 AD destruction of Temple, ~33 AD crucifixion, etc.) is >99% over a 2000-year window. Right now detail_evidence asserts 'falls within noise' qualitatively; quoting the explicit ~99% would prevent a defender from claiming the noise-floor argument is hand-waved.
sources: Hare et al. 2018 GRL DOI 10.1002/2017GL076007 for recurrence period; Campuzano et al. 2019 EPSL DOI 10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.046 for archaeomagnetic dating uncertainty

stronger_arguments.description: Note the asymmetry: SAA-like features at ~400-700 AD (Hare) and ~950 AD (Campuzano) are well-attested, but the dome only labels the one nearest 1000 AD as 'CONFIRMED'. If the same standard were applied uniformly, the dome would have to either (a) claim the ~400-700 episode also matches some theological event (which it doesn't single out), (b) claim the ~950 episode is uniquely tied to Revelation 20 (which requires a mechanism it doesn't have), or (c) acknowledge that selection bias is doing all the work. This is a stronger argument than 'unfalsifiable' alone — it shows the dome uses recurrence asymmetry inconsistently across its own claims.

kernel_of_truth.description: The ~950 AD reverse-flux-patch emergence is genuine, peer-reviewed paleomagnetic science (Campuzano et al. 2019 SHAWQ2k, EPSL DOI 10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.046, cited inline in (data/wins.json:detail_evidence)). The dome correctly identifies a real geomagnetic event and is right that the South Atlantic Anomaly is a genuinely interesting geophysical feature where active research continues — the LLSVP-driven flux-expulsion mechanism is well-supported but not fully settled (per Hare et al. 2018 GRL DOI 10.1002/2017GL076007, also cited in (data/wins.json:detail_evidence)). The current finding gives the dome credit for this directly: 'the dome correctly identified a real paleomagnetic event' (data/wins.json:finding).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a real published scientific finding is not predicting it — the dome lifted the ~950 AD date from Campuzano 2019, which predates the dome model's incorporation of WIN-023 (data/wins.json:detail_evidence). More fundamentally, the dome supplies no physical mechanism connecting any dome parameter (disc_radius, firmament_height, aetheric pressure) to the date — its own 06-evolution.txt admits failure to mathematically reconcile SAA dynamics (raw-text/06-evolution.txt). And per the noise-floor calculation, the ~50-year gap between ~950 AD and ~1000 AD falls inside the joint recurrence-and-dating uncertainty (data/wins.json:detail_evidence sentence on Hare-derived recurrence intervals), so any of several recurrences over the past two millennia could have been claimed as a match. Real-event-recognition + post-hoc theological overlay + statistically near-certain match window = retrodiction dressed as prediction, which is exactly the pattern Part 3.1b (sections.json:#posthoc-standard) is built to catch.

deeper_analysis: WIN-023 is one of four Unfalsifiable WINs (023, 031, 032, 034) that the verdict-taxonomy holistic check has flagged as borderline-theological. After the EXP-132 batch standardized U-category language in detail_verdict_text, the per-WIN argument is now structurally correct: (1) post-hoc adoption of published date, (2) theological overlay without dome-derived mechanism, (3) categorical/unfalsifiable framing. The remaining failure modes are at the TLDR layer (Tower of Babel substitution) and at the structural-tag layer (monitoring='hardcoded' inconsistent with detail-text 'no domain'). Both are easily fixed and do not destabilize the verdict. The 334% drift in detail_verdict_length is fully explained by the U-category template addition (legitimate, planned, audit-trail in ISS-820 closure) and is not a signal of unreviewed substantive change — the new content is template-correct but should be marked complete in our records. Confidence 0.85 reflects strong agreement with current verdict but acknowledged tag-internal inconsistency that needs a small patch.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Imagine the smartest possible dome defender accepting our methodology principles in good faith. Their strongest rebuttal: 'You concede WIN-023 is Unfalsifiable, not Refuted, not Self-Contradicted, not even Standard-Model-Explains. So this WIN doesn't tell us the dome model is wrong — it tells us this particular claim is non-scientific. Fine. But your taxonomy treats Unfalsifiable as a debit on the dome scorecard, when by our own Part 3.1b the legitimate-novelty carve-out is open-ended: a WIN that successfully points out a real geophysical event (the ~950 AD recurrence) AND links it to a published external timeline (preterist Revelation 20) ought at minimum to count as a hypothesis-generating observation, not a debunking. Your verdict scolds the dome for noticing pattern that even your own evidence section calls a real paleomagnetic event. The fairer verdict would be Acknowledged-but-non-discriminating, treating the WIN the same way you treat WIN-024 (latitude coincidence, Misleading) — as a real coincidence that doesn't favor either model, not as a strike against the dome.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The defense rates 2/5 — mildly creative but not text-changing. It conflates 'noticing a real event' with 'predicting' it, which is exactly what Part 3.1b prohibits, and it treats the noise-floor argument as if it were merely incidental rather than load-bearing (the ~99% any-hit probability means no inferential weight remains regardless of whether we call the verdict Unfalsifiable or Misleading). Our existing verdict-text already addresses the conflation by quoting the dome author's own home-page definition and contrasting it with our stricter standard — the defense is preempted in the second paragraph of detail_verdict_text. No change recommended; the rating is borderline 2-vs-3. If we wanted the absolute strongest preemption, we could add one sentence to detail_verdict_text saying 'noticing a real event is not the same as deriving its timing from the framework's own equations,' but the existing text already conveys this.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-004 (Std Model Explains), WIN-005 (Std Model Explains), WIN-024 (Misleading), WIN-035 (Std Model Explains), and WIN-037 (Misleading) all treat the SAA as a real, measurable, published feature whose existence is not in question — exactly as WIN-023 does. None of these WINs claim the SAA is a dome-only or aether-only phenomenon. The cross-WIN treatment is internally consistent: the dome cluster gets credit for noticing the SAA exists (kernel of truth across all five) and gets dinged for failing to derive any specific dome-mechanism prediction (consistent verdict logic across all five). One subtle tension: WIN-023 says monitoring='hardcoded' while WIN-035 (also SAA-related) says monitoring='none'. Given that WIN-023's situation per detail-text matches WIN-035's situation (no domain, just a confirmed-status assertion), the WIN-023 tag is the outlier and should be 'none' per the moderate hole flagged above. After patching WIN-023 to 'none', the SAA-cluster's monitoring tags become uniform-by-WIN-status (the ones with actual NOAA/INTERMAGNET fetches stay 'hardcoded', the ones with bare assertions become 'none').

claims_checked: detail_evidence sentence: 'Given SAA recurrence intervals of ~200-400 years and archaeomagnetic dating uncertainty of ±50-100 years, the 50-year gap between ~950 AD and the theological ~1000 AD target falls within noise — any of several recurrences could have been claimed as a match.'
Cross-WIN tag: code_analysis.monitoring='hardcoded' vs detail_verdict_text 'no validation domain'.
quantitative_verification.computations: Computed P(any paleomagnetic SAA-like recurrence falls within ±50 yr of an arbitrary pre-chosen date over a 2000-yr window) using p_per_recurrence = (target_window + 2*dating_sigma) / mean_period = (50 + 75 + 75) / 300 = 0.667 per recurrence; n=5 recurrences in 2000 yr → P(at least one hit) = 1 - (1 - 0.667)^5 ≈ 0.996. This validates the qualitative 'falls within noise' claim and quantifies it: the ~99% any-hit probability shows the ~950-vs-~1000-AD coincidence carries near-zero Bayesian weight regardless of theology. Verified separately: archaeomagnetic dating sigma per Campuzano et al. 2019 is roughly ±50-75 yr for the SHAWQ2k 1000-yr-window reconstructions; recurrence period from Hare 2018 GRL spans ~250-400 yr depending on which African Iron Age episodes are included. Both inputs are within the ranges our text uses.

citations_verified: Campuzano et al. 2019 — DOI 10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.046 — EPSL SHAWQ2k paper, correct journal and topic (verified in c2, no change since)
Tarduno et al. 2015 — DOI 10.1038/ncomms8865 — Nature Communications SAA / inner core nucleation paper, correct (verified in c2, no change since)

Hare et al. 2018 — DOI 10.1002/2017GL076007 — GRL 'New Archeomagnetic Directional Records From Iron Age Southern Africa' — DOI is now correct in current text. The c2 hole flagged this DOI as discrepant; web verification this cycle confirms 10.1002/2017GL076007 is the canonical GRL DOI for the Hare paper and the correct citation has been applied. The c1-recommended swap to 10.1073/pnas.1809197115 was actually pointing to a DIFFERENT paper (Trindade et al. 2018 PNAS speleothem record), so it would have introduced a citation error if applied; the actually-implemented fix found the right GRL DOI independently. Resolution stands: citation correct; near-miss documented.
